---
title: "Ruiz Equilibration"
date: 2026-04-18
categories: [math]
tags: [linear algebra, sparse, optimization, least squares]
---

Ruiz Equilibration is a technique to scale the Hessian matrix before using it in a quadratic program in OSQP.

1. Problem: scale a matrix so both row norms and column norms are balanced, targeting infinity norm of 1 for each.
2. Method: iterative diagonal scaling on both sides of the matrix, using row and column infinity norms at each step.
3. Update idea:
    - Build diagonal matrices from square roots of current row/column infinity norms.
    - Apply inverse left and right scaling to the next matrix.
    - Accumulate total scaling factors in two diagonal matrices.
4. Stopping condition: maximum deviation of row/column infinity norms from 1 is at most epsilon.
5. Main claim: convergence is at least linear, with asymptotic rate of 1/2.
6. Context: connects to HSL routines MC29 and MC30, symmetry is naturally preserved in the symmetric case; discusses possible extensions to one-norm and two-norm settings.
